headline: Upcoming G7 puts serious strain on relations between France and Switzerland
description: The summit, scheduled to take place in Evian from June 15 to 17, has required the involvement of Swiss authorities due to delegations transiting through Geneva Airport and a planned demonstration in the city. Tensions have run high on the Swiss side, where outbreaks of violence occurred in 2003 on the sidelines of a previous summit.

headline: Paris welcomes PSG home to celebrate back-to-back Champions League victories
Outgoing chair Powell delivers defense of Fed independence
A Trumpist makes it through to Colombia presidential runoff
description: Abelardo de la Espriella, 47, a pro-Trump lawyer who calls himself 'The Tiger,' has vowed a 'shock plan' to bombard armed groups, echoing the iron-fist rhetoric that has swept the right to power across Latin America.

headline: Sweden ramps up aid to households and businesses to tackle energy crisis
description: Less than four months before the legislative elections, the liberal conservative right and the far right have announced 17.3 billion kronor (approximately €1.6 billion) worth of support measures, including halving the price of tickets for public transportation.

headline: Is it ever a good idea to... wear a short-sleeved shirt?
description: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or else, to do so deliberately.

headline: The Chinese women striving to escape societal pressures to marry and have children
description: From climbing gyms to specialty bookstores and entrepreneur clubs, several women-focused spaces have opened in China's major cities. These places of solidarity offer women ways to push back against pressures to have children in a country where feminist activism is closely monitored.

Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
description: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

headline: Paul McCartney blends rock and melancholic ballads in 'The Boys of Dungeon Lane,' his 20th solo album
description: The Beatles' co-founder invited the band's drummer, Ringo Starr, for a duet on the album. Starr, meanwhile, released his own record in April.

headline: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
description: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.
